at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.title: maximizing videos without stretching
at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: this seems to be a regression from a few commits ago. maximizing a video to fullscreen modifies the aspect ratio, making it incredibly annoying to view video content.

at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: something broke in a recent commit that causes videos to stretch when maximised. video aspect ratio must be preserved.

at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.title: typeahead for handle inputs
at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: we have handle inputs in several locations: - when adding users to a knot - when adding collaborators to a repo - when adding a DID-formatted label value it would be nice to have handle type-ahead with a profile picture to speed things up.

at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.title: return a link from knotserver to quickly compare new branches against default branch
at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: when a new branch is pushed to a git repository on a knot, return a `/compare` link so users can open a PR with 2 clicks. the logic is roughly: - detect new branches (this can be detected in the hooks as a ref where the hash moves from `000...` to `someHash` - for every new ref,…

at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.title: look into incorrect diffs produced by patchutil.CombineDiff
at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: the algorithm is slightly wrong, or needs to handle an edge-case: it sometimes adds empty lines for lines that are unknown and these are rendered in the final diff. it remains to be seen if CombinePatch is even possible without a git-index, one [fix](https://tangled.org/@tangled…

at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.title: stack ordering can be incorrect
at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: the order of the stack should strictly be according to the linked list. in some components, the ordering is off and is sorted by time of creation.

at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.title: some PR resubmits do not advance a round
at://did:plc:qfpnj4og54vl56wngdriaxug/sh.body_excerpt: in stacked PRs, there is an optimization to avoid resubmitting a PR if nothing about the patch changed. this is not preferable for several reasons: the contents of the patch being identical does not mean that the code behaviour is identical. this behaviour should be undone.
